title: Init --global-only preserves an existing project config
description: Black-box smoke for `armature init --global-only` when the project config already exists.
input: Seed an existing project `.armature.json`, run `armature init --global-only`, and verify the project config content remains unchanged while the global config still exists.
command:
bash -lc 'REPO_ROOT=${PROJECT_DIR:-$(pwd)}; cd "$REPO_ROOT"; bash agent-eval/scripts/prepare-fast-gate.sh >/dev/null; TMP_HOME=$(mktemp -d); TMP_PROJECT=$(mktemp -d); trap "rm -rf $TMP_HOME $TMP_PROJECT" EXIT; mkdir -p "$TMP_HOME/.armature"; cat > "$TMP_PROJECT/.armature.json" <<EOF
{"providers":{"poe":{"apiKey":"keep-project"}},"defaultProvider":"poe"}
EOF
OUTPUT=$(cd "$TMP_PROJECT" && env -i PATH="$PATH" HOME="$TMP_HOME" ARMATURE_HOME="$TMP_HOME/.armature" node "$REPO_ROOT/dist/bin/armature.js" init --global-only); printf "%s\n" "$OUTPUT"; test -f "$TMP_HOME/.armature/config.json"; grep -q "keep-project" "$TMP_PROJECT/.armature.json"; printf "init-global-only-preserve-ok\n"'
